repair_candidate.summary: Rebase and repair the existing maintainer-editable contributor PR #87799 against current main without broadening it. Preserve the two-file installer stdin-isolation fix in scripts/install.sh and test/scripts/install-sh.test.ts, resolve only current-main compatibility conflicts, validate the changed surface, and leave merge/finalization to a later authorized lane. repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(install): harden stdin consumers to prevent pipe corruption in curl | bash
repair_candidate.pr_body:
What Problem This Solves

Repairs the existing #87799 contributor branch after it became stale against current main. The underlying user-facing issue is installer pipe corruption in the documented curl | bash flow, tracked by #90008 and duplicate context #73814.

Why This Change Was Made

The branch should keep the existing narrow approach: harden installer subprocess stdin consumers instead of changing the website command or adding a top-level pipe guard. Earlier attempt #73830 was closed because it did not solve child-process stdin inheritance. Earlier compatibility-risk context #82918 is not executable repair lineage for this artifact because it carries merge-risk: compatibility.

User Impact

Piped installs should avoid child processes consuming the installer script stream, while direct interactive installs should retain terminal stdin for prompts.

Evidence

Rebase onto main 04865fe44b4c8521cdd1fa72fb26f08b6e201b4d, resolve only installer-surface conflicts, run pnpm check:changed -- scripts/install.sh test/scripts/install-sh.test.ts, run pnpm test test/scripts/install-sh.test.ts, and run Codex /review in the executor before any later merge finalization. Credit stays with @SebTardif and source PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87799.
credit_notes: Repair the existing contributor branch for https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87799 rather than replacing it.
Preserve @SebTardif's authorship and credit for the installer stdin-isolation fix.
Do not add a replacement PR or broaden the installer change beyond the existing scripts/install.sh and test/scripts/install-sh.test.ts intent.
Do not use #82918 as source PR or executable lineage because it carries the blocked merge-risk: compatibility label; it remains historical context only.
